name: Iterative BFS (Queue)
summary: Level-order walk with an explicit queue; swap each dequeued node's children.
visualizationIntent: init: build and show the input tree; an empty queue aux sits beside it, then the root id enters it. loop: dequeue the front node (cur) — a cursor ring lands on it and it turns visited (blue); its two children swap positions with a glide; its swapped children are enqueued and turn frontier (amber), so the queue visibly grows then drains level by level. return: once the queue empties the whole tree is mirrored; serialize it back to level-order into the result strip.

lineExplanations.3: Count how many array slots describe the tree.
lineExplanations.4: Start an empty list of real node objects for the drawing.
lineExplanations.5: Start a lookup from a node's id to its object.
lineExplanations.6: Start a per-node state tracker: unseen, queued, or visited.
lineExplanations.7: Loop over every slot to initialize its state.
lineExplanations.9: Point the child-slot walker just past the root.
lineExplanations.10: Loop over every slot to build the node objects.
lineExplanations.11: Only non-null slots become real nodes.
lineExplanations.12: Create this node with its value and two empty child links.
lineExplanations.13: Is there a slot left to be the left child?
lineExplanations.14: Is that candidate slot a real (non-null) node?
lineExplanations.15: Link that slot as this node's left child.
lineExplanations.16: Advance the walker past the left-child slot.
lineExplanations.17: Is there a slot left to be the right child?
lineExplanations.18: Is that candidate slot a real (non-null) node?
lineExplanations.19: Link that slot as this node's right child.
lineExplanations.20: Advance the walker past the right-child slot.
lineExplanations.21: Add the finished node to the tree.
lineExplanations.22: Record the node under its id for O(1) lookup.
lineExplanations.24: No node is being visited yet (-1 = none).
lineExplanations.25: Seed the queue with the root when the tree is non-empty.
lineExplanations.26: Enqueue the root id to start the sweep.
lineExplanations.28: Keep sweeping while the queue holds nodes.
lineExplanations.29: Dequeue the front node — the one to invert now.
lineExplanations.33: Make the old right child the new left child.
lineExplanations.34: Make the old left child the new right child.
lineExplanations.35: Does it have a (swapped-in) left child?
lineExplanations.36: Enqueue that left child for the next level.
lineExplanations.38: Does it have a (swapped-in) right child?
lineExplanations.39: Enqueue that right child for the next level.
lineExplanations.41: Sweep done — clear the current-node cursor.
lineExplanations.42: Start an empty output list for the level-order result.
lineExplanations.43: Start a queue to walk the inverted tree for serialization.
lineExplanations.44: Seed the walk with the root when the tree is non-empty.
lineExplanations.45: Put the root id into the serialization queue.
lineExplanations.46: Keep emitting while ids remain to serialize.
lineExplanations.47: Take the next id from the front of the walk.
lineExplanations.48: A None entry marks a missing child.
lineExplanations.49: Emit null for that missing child.
lineExplanations.51: Emit this node's value into the result.
lineExplanations.52: Queue its left child to emit next.
lineExplanations.53: Queue its right child after that.
lineExplanations.54: Trim trailing nulls the level-order format omits.
lineExplanations.56: Return the inverted tree in level-order form.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: the wrapper class LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def invertTree(self, values): the entry method; values is the tree as a level-order array.
syntaxExplanations.3: len(values) counts the elements in the input array.
syntaxExplanations.5: by_id = {} creates an empty dictionary (a map from id to node).
syntaxExplanations.6: state_by_id = [] will hold one status number per node id.
syntaxExplanations.7: for i in range(n): loops i from 0 to n-1.
syntaxExplanations.8: append(0) adds a 0 (unseen) entry for this slot.
syntaxExplanations.9: child = 1 sets the next-child pointer to slot 1.
syntaxExplanations.10: for i in range(n): a second pass, this time to build nodes.
syntaxExplanations.11: is not None checks the slot holds a real value.
syntaxExplanations.12: A dict literal { ... } builds this node object.
syntaxExplanations.13: if child < n: is there still a slot available?
syntaxExplanations.14: Check the candidate child slot is not null.
syntaxExplanations.15: node["left"] = child stores the left child's id.
syntaxExplanations.16: child = child + 1 advances to the next slot.
syntaxExplanations.17: if child < n: is there another slot for the right child?
syntaxExplanations.18: Check the candidate right slot is not null.
syntaxExplanations.19: node["right"] = child stores the right child's id.
syntaxExplanations.21: nodes.append(node) adds the built node to the list.
syntaxExplanations.22: by_id[i] = node registers the node under its id.
syntaxExplanations.23: queue = [] is a list used as a FIFO queue.
syntaxExplanations.24: cur = -1 means no node is current yet.
syntaxExplanations.25: if n > 0: only sweep a non-empty tree.
syntaxExplanations.28: while len(queue) > 0: keep going until the queue empties.
syntaxExplanations.29: pop(0) removes and returns the front item (dequeue).
syntaxExplanations.31: Read the current left-child id into left.
syntaxExplanations.32: Read the current right-child id into right.
syntaxExplanations.33: Assign right into the left slot.
syntaxExplanations.34: Assign left into the right slot — the swap is done.
syntaxExplanations.35: is not None checks a real left child exists.
syntaxExplanations.36: append(...) enqueues the left child id.
syntaxExplanations.38: is not None checks a real right child exists.
syntaxExplanations.39: append(...) enqueues the right child id.
syntaxExplanations.41: cur = -1 clears the cursor after the sweep.
syntaxExplanations.42: result = [] will collect the level-order output.
syntaxExplanations.43: out_q = [] is a list used as a queue.
syntaxExplanations.44: if n > 0: only walk a non-empty tree.
syntaxExplanations.46: while len(out_q) > 0: keep going until the queue empties.
syntaxExplanations.47: pop(0) removes and returns the front item (dequeue).
syntaxExplanations.48: if emit_id is None: this slot is a missing child.
syntaxExplanations.49: append(None) records a null hole in the output.
syntaxExplanations.50: else: the slot is a real node.
syntaxExplanations.52: append(..."left"...) enqueues the left child id.
syntaxExplanations.53: append(..."right"...) enqueues the right child id.
syntaxExplanations.54: while ... is None: trim trailing null placeholders.
syntaxExplanations.56: return result hands back the inverted tree.
